step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1's conclusion. Biofilms on teeth are indeed referred to as dental plaque because they form a sticky film composed primarily of bacteria adhering to tooth surfaces, leading to potential dental caries if not removed regularly through brushing and flossing. This aligns well with the definition provided earlier about biofilms being communities of microorganisms attached to surfaces. 

Answer: C Your agreement adds an important point by emphasizing how regular removal prevents more serious issues like cavities, reinforcing why dental plaque specifically fits the description given. Well reasoned!
